excerpt: A Muslim is one who yields entirely to divine command and recognizes the binding authority of the Imams, who distribute the Sacred Law and its esoteric meaning to their congregations.
source_location_hint: Introduction to works on the Imams and Sacred Law

excerpt: The Imam of the age is the Timekeeper who, through his concealment, determines the span of human life by his luminous form, and Ramadan witnesses a particular descent of divine mercy wherein sustenance is apportioned and each soul's term is inscribed.
source_location_hint: Teachings on the Imam's role in cosmic governance

excerpt: Indeed, to Allah belong origination and will in all things, excepting the Imamate—the Imam holds complete authority in creation, generation, transformation, increase, and diminishment save in the principle of succession.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of divine will and Imamate

excerpt: Whosoever fulfills one obligation in Ramadan shall have fulfilled seventy; and whoever provides sustenance to a fasting person, Allah shall forgive his sins and liberate him from Hellfire.
source_location_hint: Laws of fasting and charity in Ramadan

excerpt: In the testimony 'there is no god but Allah,' both the alif and the lam are mentioned five times: five paired with five, yielding twenty-five; there are ten praises of Allah which are five doubled.
source_location_hint: Numerical and linguistic symbolism in Islamic devotion
